industries.name: Financial Services
industries.description: Banks, insurers, asset managers, and fintechs operating in one of the most targeted and heavily regulated sectors in cybersecurity.
challenges: High-value targets for financially motivated attackers and nation-state actors
DORA (Digital Operational Resilience Act) in force from January 2025 — mandatory ICT risk management, incident reporting, and third-party oversight
PCI DSS v4.0.1 — all future-dated requirements mandatory from March 2025
Legacy systems that cannot easily be patched or replaced
Third-party and supply chain risk from extensive ecosystem dependencies

industries.name: Healthcare
industries.description: Hospitals, clinics, medical device manufacturers, and health-tech companies protecting some of the most sensitive personal data in existence.
challenges: Patient data protection under HIPAA (US) and NIS2 (EU, transposed October 2024)
Medical device security under updated FDA cybersecurity guidance and IEC 62443
OT/IoT risk from connected clinical devices with long lifecycles and no patch path
Clinical system availability requirements — ransomware remains the leading cause of healthcare disruption
Ransomware groups specifically targeting healthcare for maximum extortion leverage

industries.name: Government
industries.description: Public sector organizations managing critical infrastructure, citizen data, and national security requirements.
challenges: Nation-state threat actors with sophisticated capabilities — cyber operations increasingly linked to geopolitical conflict
NIS2 Directive (EU, transposed October 2024) — mandatory security requirements for public sector entities
Complex procurement and accreditation requirements (FedRAMP, NCSC Cyber Essentials Plus, NIST RMF)
Aging IT infrastructure with limited modernisation budgets
Insider threat risk in sensitive environments

industries.name: Education
industries.description: Universities, schools, and ed-tech platforms balancing open academic environments with protection of student and research data.
challenges: Open network environments that conflict with security controls — segmentation without disrupting academic freedom
Large attack surfaces from diverse student, staff, and BYOD research devices
Research data and intellectual property targeted by nation-state actors
Ransomware increasingly targeting schools and universities for extortion
Limited security budget relative to complexity — often no dedicated CISO

industries.name: Manufacturing
industries.description: Industrial manufacturers with operational technology environments that were never designed with cybersecurity in mind.
challenges: OT/IT convergence introducing cyber risk to production environments — IEC 62443 the governing standard
Legacy industrial control systems (ICS/SCADA) with no vendor patch path
Supply chain compromise as a primary entry vector — software and hardware supply chain both targeted
Business continuity requirements limiting maintenance windows and patching
NIS2 Directive (EU) imposing mandatory security requirements on critical infrastructure operators

industries.name: Retail
industries.description: Retailers and e-commerce businesses protecting payment card data, customer information, and high-availability transaction systems.
challenges: PCI DSS v4.0.1 compliance — all future-dated requirements mandatory from March 2025; tighter requirements on authentication, scripts, and e-skimming controls
E-commerce application security — Magecart/web skimming attacks targeting payment pages remain active
Seasonal traffic spikes that complicate security operations and patching windows
Third-party integrations and supply chain risk from payment processors and logistics partners
